summary: Audited 25 .accessibilityLabel/.accessibilityValue/.accessibilityHint call sites in the Owlory app target. 12 already localized (literal keys or helper-routed via writeRowAccessibilityHint, continueAccessibilityHint, readinessScaleAccessibilityLabel, trainingStatusAccessibilityLabel). 10 real gaps grouped into A (Home task and protocol-step action labels), B (VoiceCaptureButton + AudioPlaybackButton state phrases), C (Train readiness scale row labels — mirrors today.readiness.scale.accessibility shape), D (BuildInfoView label:value diagnostic line, low priority). 1 system-generated (TrainView.swift:499 .accessibilityValue with a bare integer). Group A is queued as app-localization-home-action-accessibility-formatting; Groups B/C/D are documented in localization-string-inventory.md but not queued. Doc-only; no Swift source touched.

contract_status_changes.before: The localization-string-inventory deferred bucket listed 'remaining model-backed accessibility interpolation outside today.readiness.scale.accessibility' as unaudited. No inventory existed of which accessibility-modifier sites were unlocalized.
contract_status_changes.after: All 25 accessibility-modifier sites in the app target are classified. The doc lists 10 real gaps in four thematic groups with concrete file/line citations. Group A is queued as a follow-up implementation slice; B/C/D are documented but unqueued, awaiting prioritization.

residual_risks: Groups B, C, and D from the audit are documented but not queued. They remain concrete gaps until prioritized into their own scoped slices.
The audit only inspects the Owlory app target. Widget extension and test targets are excluded; they have separate accessibility concerns.

Static-analysis only: the audit relies on call-site shape (literal vs variable vs helper call) to classify localized-ness. A future regression that converts a literal `Text` literal to a non-LocalizedStringKey String variable would silently bypass localization without showing up as a new accessibility-modifier site.
No UI surface was exercised; the audit does not prove any of the already-localized sites actually render correctly under non-English locales.
recommended_next_reason: Group A is the highest-leverage follow-up: it covers user-visible Home task and protocol-step action accessibility (Edit/Skip/Mark complete/Mark incomplete/Restore/Complete step/Skip step), and the strings are clustered in a single file with a clean format-string shape (%@ for the title interpolation). The slice is already classified in the queue with allowed_paths, validations, and an entry condition tied to this audit.
